name: Italy
description: Italy is a southern European country spanning the Alps, the Mediterranean, and a long peninsula, drawing remote earners and retirees for its climate, cities, and special tax regimes. The figures below are screened, sourced, and dated. They are not advice.

priceLevelIndexEU27.sourceName: Eurostat, price level index for actual individual consumption (tec00120, EU27=100)
priceLevelIndexEU27.excerpt: Italy price level index for actual individual consumption (EU27 = 100): 97.1 in 2025 (100 = EU average).

averageJanuaryHighC.sourceName: Rome climate normals (NOAA 1991-2020, Ciampino), via Wikipedia
averageJanuaryHighC.excerpt: Rome, shown as representative for Italy: mean daily maximum 12.0 C in January (1991 to 2020 normals).

averageJulyHighC.sourceName: Rome climate normals (NOAA 1991-2020, Ciampino), via Wikipedia
averageJulyHighC.excerpt: Rome: mean daily maximum 31.0 C in July (1991 to 2020 normals).

averageAnnualSunHours.sourceName: Rome climate normals (NOAA 1991-2020, Ciampino), via Wikipedia
averageAnnualSunHours.excerpt: Rome: about 2,723.9 mean annual sunshine hours (1991 to 2020 normals).

koppenClass.sourceName: GloH2O Koppen-Geiger climate classification (Beck et al. 2023)
koppenClass.excerpt: Koppen-Geiger class Csa, hot-summer Mediterranean, dominant across central and southern Italy including Rome.

digitalNomadVisa.sourceName: Consolato Generale d'Italia Boston, digital nomad / remote worker visa
digitalNomadVisa.excerpt: the minimum legal income is no less than 24,789 euros (or the equivalent in USD) per year, about 2,066 euros per month.

goldenVisa.value: Investor visa with four routes: 250,000 euros in an Italian innovative startup; 500,000 euros in an Italian limited company; 1,000,000 euros in a philanthropic initiative; or 2,000,000 euros in Italian government bonds.
goldenVisa.sourceName: Investor Visa for Italy, official portal (Ministry of Enterprises and Made in Italy)
goldenVisa.excerpt: Investment routes: 250,000 euros in an innovative startup, 500,000 euros in a limited company, 1,000,000 euros in a philanthropic initiative, or 2,000,000 euros in government bonds.

headlinePersonalIncomeTaxRate.sourceName: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries, Italy, taxes on personal income
headlinePersonalIncomeTaxRate.excerpt: National income tax top rate 43 percent on taxable income over 50,000 euros, plus regional (1.23 to 3.33 percent) and municipal (0 to 0.9 percent) surcharges.

specialRegime.value: Flat substitute tax for new residents on foreign-source income: 200,000 euros per year for those opting from the 2025 tax period, rising to 300,000 euros for residence transfers from 1 January 2026, plus 25,000 euros per family member. Foreign pensioners who move to a small southern municipality can instead elect a 7 percent flat tax for up to 10 years, and an inbound-workers (impatriati) regime gives a partial income exemption.
specialRegime.sourceName: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries, Italy, taxes on personal income
specialRegime.excerpt: For the individuals who opt for the lump sum tax regime from the 2025 tax period, the flat tax amounts to EUR 200,000; for individuals who transfer their legal residency to Italy starting from 1 January 2026 the flat tax is increased from EUR 200,000 to EUR 300,000.

physiciansPer1000.sourceName: WHO Global Health Observatory, medical doctors per 10,000, via Wikipedia
physiciansPer1000.excerpt: Italy: 42.5 medical doctors per 10,000 (about 4.25 per 1,000), WHO 2022.

peaceIndexScore.sourceName: Institute for Economics and Peace, Global Peace Index 2024
peaceIndexScore.excerpt: Italy 2024 Global Peace Index score 1.692 (rank 33 of 163). Lower is more peaceful.
